commonCitations.medical_records_fees_hospital: Cal. Health & Safety Code § 123110 (patient access — up to $0.25/page standard, $0.50/page oversized, plus reasonable staff retrieval at $16 per 15 minutes)
commonCitations.medical_records_fees_physician: Cal. Health & Safety Code § 123110 (same cap applies to physicians and other providers)
commonCitations.minor_settlement_approval: Cal. Prob. Code § 3500 et seq.; Cal. Code Civ. Proc. § 372; Cal. R. Ct. 7.950–7.958 (minor's compromise — court approval required)
commonCitations.tort_claims_act_notice: Cal. Gov. Code §§ 910, 911.2 (Government Claims Act — content of claim and time to present)
commonCitations.tort_claims_act_deadline_days: 180 (6 months for PI/property claims under Gov. Code § 911.2(a); 1 year for other causes — confirm category before filing)
commonCitations.contingency_fee_rule: Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code § 6147 (written agreement required); Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code § 6146 (medical malpractice sliding-scale cap)
commonCitations.certificate_of_merit_medmal: Cal. Code Civ. Proc. § 364 (90-day pre-suit notice to healthcare provider — no formal certificate, but notice is a statutory prerequisite)
commonCitations.damages_cap_medmal_noneconomic: Cal. Civ. Code § 3333.2 (post-AB 35 tiered caps — 2026: $470,000 injury / $650,000 wrongful death; escalating $40k/$50k annually through 2034, then 2% CPI adjustment; cap pegged to year cause of action arose — earlier-year injuries apply lower caps)
commonCitations.comparative_negligence_standard: Pure comparative negligence — Li v. Yellow Cab Co., 13 Cal. 3d 804, 829 (1975) (plaintiff's recovery reduced by percentage of fault; no bar)
commonCitations.threshold_serious_injury: N/A (California is an at-fault jurisdiction; no serious-injury threshold for PI claims)
